message:
feat(vdbe): FusedAppendInsert superinstruction — handler + peephole codegen

V2.1: Complete implementation of the highest-EV optimization remaining.
Fuses NewRowid + MakeRecord + Insert into a single FusedAppendInsert
superinstruction that bypasses the interpreter dispatch loop.

Three components:
1. Opcode::FusedAppendInsert (194) defined in opcode.rs with full
   documentation of semantics, register layout, and guard conditions.

2. Hot-path handler in engine.rs that:
   - Allocates rowid via cached last_alloc_rowid (no B-tree traversal)
   - Serializes record directly from registers into sideband buffer
   - Appends to B-tree via table_insert (prechecked absent mode)
   - Updates all bookkeeping (changes, last_insert_rowid, etc.)

3. Peephole optimizer in lib.rs ProgramBuilder::finish() that scans the
   opcode stream for the pattern [NewRowid(C,R_rowid), MakeRecord(R_start,
   N,R_record), Insert(C,R_record,R_rowid)] and replaces with
   FusedAppendInsert(C,R_start,N,flags) + 2x Noop.

Guard conditions: only fuses when all three reference the same cursor,
register operands match, and Insert uses OE_ABORT (2) conflict mode.
Tables with indexes, triggers, FKs, or non-ABORT conflict modes use
the normal unfused opcodes.

Expected impact: eliminates 2 dispatch cycles per INSERT row (~10-15ns)
plus all intermediate register reads/writes (~50-100ns).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
